answer.recommended_action: Inspect the TEC thermal path to isolate the elevated thermal resistance: (1) measure heat-sink temperature independently to determine whether degradation is in the TEC pump itself or in downstream heat dissipation; (2) clean or replace heat-sink thermal interface material and verify fan/airflow is unobstructed; (3) if heat-sink temperature is nominal but TEC cold-junction remains above setpoint with 18% current headroom, the TEC module efficiency has degraded — replace TEC. Do NOT simply raise TEC current limit; headroom exists but setpoint cannot be held, confirming thermal-path resistance is the bottleneck, not drive capacity. After remediation, re-verify diode temperature against the 26.0°C setpoint and re-measure effective range to confirm full recovery. Separately evaluate H1, H3, and H5 to account for any residual range loss beyond the ~5.5% laser-power contribution.

hypotheses.label: Window / optics contamination reducing transmit/receive throughput
Laser output power aging or degradation reducing emitted intensity
Detector responsivity drift reducing sensitivity at range margin
TEC / thermal regulation failure causing temperature-dependent SNR loss
Calibration or firmware drift shifting range-detection threshold

evidence.summary: Laser output 45.4 mW vs 48.1 mW baseline (−5.5% decline confirmed). However, diode temperature 28.2 °C exceeds setpoint maximum of 26.0 °C; the check attributes the power reduction to thermal over-drive, NOT laser aging. This partially (but not fully) accounts for the ~8% range loss and redirects causation toward thermal regulation rather than emitter degradation.

TEC current is 2.04 A — 82% of the 2.5 A limit — indicating significant sustained thermal drive, yet the diode temperature remains at 28.2 °C, which is 2.2 °C above the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum (losing_setpoint=true). Crucially, the TEC still has current headroom (~18%) but cannot hold setpoint; this rules out simple power-supply starvation and instead implicates TEC thermal-path degradation (e.g., reduced pump efficiency, elevated thermal resistance at the cold junction, or rising heat-sink temperature). This result corroborates ev.laser_power_check_01's thermal attribution of the 5.5% laser power drop and confirms TEC/thermal regulation failure (H4) as the root cause of the ~8% range degradation.
log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

No evidence exists yet; all hypotheses sit at equal prior odds. The single sharpest first cut is a direct measurement of emitted laser output power. If power is nominal → H2 (laser aging) is strongly argued AGAINST and weight shifts toward H1/H3/H4/H5. If power is measurably low (consistent with ~8% range loss, which maps to a larger fractional power drop depending on the range equation exponent) → H2 is strongly supported and H1/H3/H4/H5 are relatively disfavoured. This is a transmit-side vs. receive-side / optics / calibration split — the broadest single discriminator available before any evidence is in hand. A stuck or near-zero-variance readout on the power channel would also flag a lying sensor (channel sanity concern), which would redirect to channel_sanity_check next.

ev.laser_power_check_01 establishes that diode temperature (28.2 °C) exceeds the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum and attributes the −5.5% laser power drop to thermal over-drive, making H4 (TEC/thermal regulation failure) the current leading hypothesis. However, the laser power loss alone accounts for only ~5.5% of the ~8% range degradation, leaving a gap that H3 (detector responsivity drift) or H1 (optics contamination) could fill. tec_load_check will directly interrogate TEC duty cycle, current draw, and regulation error. If TEC is saturated or faulted, H4 is confirmed as the dominant cause and H1/H3/H5 recede. If TEC load is nominal, the thermal elevation must originate upstream (elevated ambient, coolant, or shared power rail — hyp.upstream_power) and H4 as a discrete TEC fault is weakened, redirecting focus. Either result materially separates the leading hypotheses from one another rather than re-confirming already-held evidence.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
